transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 60 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 69 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 72 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 73 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1410 of file websocket/transport.c.

1411                                                                                        {
1412   if (!name) {
1413     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1414     return NULL;
1415   }
1416 
1417   if (!url) {
1418     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1419     return NULL;
1420   }
1421 
1422   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1423   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1424   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1425   if (!protocol_end) {
1426     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1427     return NULL;
1428   }
1429 
1430   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1431   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1432 
1433   // Find port (if specified)
1434   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1435   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1436 
1437   char host[256] = {0};
1438   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1439   char path[256] = "/";
1440 
1441   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1442     // Port is specified
1443     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1444     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1445       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1446       return NULL;
1447     }
1448     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1449     host[host_len] = '\0';
1450 
1451     // Extract port
1452     char *endptr;
1453     errno = 0;
1454     long port_val = strtol(port_start + 1, &endptr, 10);
1455     if (*endptr != '\0' || errno != 0 || port_val <= 0 || port_val > 65535) {
1456       log_error("websocket_transport_create: Invalid port number: %s", port_start + 1);
1457       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1458       return NULL;
1459     }
1460     port = (uint16_t)port_val;
1461   } else {
1462     // No port specified, use default
1463     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1464     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1465       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1466       return NULL;
1467     }
1468     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1469     host[host_len] = '\0';
1470   }
1471 
1472   // Extract path
1473   if (path_start) {
1474     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1475     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1476   }
1477 
1478   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1479 
1480   // Allocate transport structure
1481   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1482   if (!transport) {
1483     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1484     return NULL;
1485   }
1486 
1487   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1488   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1489       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1490   if (!ws_data) {
1491     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1492     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1493     return NULL;
1494   }
1495 
1496   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1497   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1498 
1499   // Create receive queue
1500   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1501   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1502     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1503     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1504     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1505     return NULL;
1506   }
1507 
1508   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1509   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1510   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1511     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1512     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1513     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1514     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1515     return NULL;
1516   }
1517 
1518   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1519   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1520   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1521   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1522 
1523   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1524     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1525     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1526     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1527     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1528     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1529     return NULL;
1530   }
1531 
1532   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1533     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1534     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1535     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1536     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1537     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1538     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1539     return NULL;
1540   }
1541 
1542   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1543     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1544     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1545     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1546     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1547     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1548     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1549     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1550     return NULL;
1551   }
1552 
1553   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1554     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1555     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1556     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1557     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1558     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1559     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1560     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1561     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1562     return NULL;
1563   }
1564 
1565   // Allocate initial send buffer
1566   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1567   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1568   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1569     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1570     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1571     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1572     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1573     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1574     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1575     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1576     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1577     return NULL;
1578   }
1579 
1580   // Create libwebsockets context
1581   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1582   // Register "http" protocol for WebSocket connections - matches server-side protocol name
1583   // This ensures libwebsockets will invoke our websocket_callback during upgrade handshake and communication
1584   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1585       {
1586           "http", // Default HTTP protocol - handles WebSocket upgrade and ACIP communication
1587           websocket_callback,
1588           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1589           524288, // RX buffer size
1590           0,      // ID
1591           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1592           524288  // TX packet size
1593       },
1594       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1595   };
1596 
1597   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1598   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1599   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1600   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1601   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1602   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1603   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1604 
1605   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1606   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1607   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1608   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1609   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1610   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1611   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1612   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1613   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1614   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1615 
1616   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1617   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1618   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1619   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1620 
1621   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1622   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1623   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1624       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1625       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1626   };
1627   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1628 
1629   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1630   lws_log_init_client();
1631 
1632   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1633 
1634   if (!ws_data->context) {
1635     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1636     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1637     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1638     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1639     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1640     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1641     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1642     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1643     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1644     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1645     return NULL;
1646   }
1647 
1648   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1649   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1650   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1651   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1652   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1653 
1654   // Connect to WebSocket server
1655   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1656   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1657   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1658   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1659   connect_info.address = host;
1660   connect_info.port = port;
1661   connect_info.path = path;
1662   connect_info.host = host;
1663   connect_info.origin = host;
1664   // Request the ACIP subprotocol so server routes to acip_protocol handler
1665   // This ensures WebSocket upgrade includes Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: acip
1666   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "acip";
1667   connect_info.protocol = "acip";
1668   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1669   connect_info.ssl_connection =
1670       use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1671   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1672 
1673   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1674   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1675   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1676   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1677     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1678     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1679     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1680     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1681     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1682     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1683     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1684     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1685     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1686     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1687     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1688     return NULL;
1689   }
1690 
1691   // Initialize transport
1692   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1693   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1694   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1695 
1696   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1697   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1698   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1699   ws_data->service_running = true;
1700   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1701     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1702     ws_data->service_running = false;
1703     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1704     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1705     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1706     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1707     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1708     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1709     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1710     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1711     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1712     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1713     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1714     return NULL;
1715   }
1716   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1717 
1718   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1719   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1720   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1721   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1722   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1723   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1724 
1725   // Register transport
1726   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1727 
1728   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1729   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1730 
1731   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1732   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1733   return transport;
1734 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::connection_failed,
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, errno, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_NETWORK, acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_destroying, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, lws_log_init_client(),
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_running,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_thread, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.


Referenced by connection_factory_open(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming
  clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1746 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1747                                                                                        {
1748   if (!name) {
1749     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1750     return NULL;
1751   }
1752 
1753   if (!wsi) {
1754     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1755     return NULL;
1756   }
1757 
1758   // Allocate transport structure
1759   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1760   if (!transport) {
1761     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1762     return NULL;
1763   }
1764 
1765   // Allocate transport-specific data
1766   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1767       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1768   if (!ws_data) {
1769     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1770     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1771     return NULL;
1772   }
1773 
1774   // Initialize receive queue
1775   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1776   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1777     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1778     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1779     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1780     return NULL;
1781   }
1782 
1783   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1784   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1785   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1786     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1787     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1788     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1789     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1790     return NULL;
1791   }
1792 
1793   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1794   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1795   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1796   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1797   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1798     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1799     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1800     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1801     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1802     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1803     return NULL;
1804   }
1805 
1806   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1807   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1808   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1809   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1810   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1811   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1812 
1813   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1814     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1815     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1816     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1817     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1818     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1819     return NULL;
1820   }
1821 
1822   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1823     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1824     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1825     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1826     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1827     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1828     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1829     return NULL;
1830   }
1831 
1832   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1833     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1834     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1835     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1836     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1837     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1838     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1839     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1840     return NULL;
1841   }
1842 
1843   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1844     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1845     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1846     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1847     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1848     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1849     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1850     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1851     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1852     return NULL;
1853   }
1854 
1855   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1856     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1857     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1858     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1859     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1860     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1861     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1862     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1863     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1864     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1865     return NULL;
1866   }
1867 
1868   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1869     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1870     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1871     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1872     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1873     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1874     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1875     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1876     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1877     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1878     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1879     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1880     return NULL;
1881   }
1882 
1883   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1884   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1885   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1886   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1887     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1888     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1889     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1890     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1891     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1892     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1893     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1894     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1895     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1896     return NULL;
1897   }
1898   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1899 
1900   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1901   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1902   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1903   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1904   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1905   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1906            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1907   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1908 
1909   // Initialize transport
1910   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1911   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1912   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1913 
1914   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1915 
1916   // Register transport
1917   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1918 
1919   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1920   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1921 
1922   return transport;
1923 }






References cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT,
    NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
